title: Tapping for the Ageing Hair
subtitle: A tapping practice for the complicated feelings that arrive when hair starts to grey or thin
excerpt: A tapping practice for the feelings that arrive when hair changes with age: the grey that comes in unexpectedly, the shift in texture, the face in the mirror that looks like someone slightly different. For use when the feelings are more complicated than expected.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the changing hair is triggering more feeling than seems proportionate, or when the mirror has become a difficult place to look.

content.text: A tapping practice for the complicated feelings that arrive when hair changes with age. Grey coming in. Texture shifting. The face in the mirror that looks like someone slightly different from the person you still feel like inside.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though my hair has changed and I do not always like what I see, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the grey feels like something is being taken from me, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I have complicated feelings about my own hair, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** My hair is changing.
**Side of eye:** The grey is coming in and the texture has shifted.
**Under eye:** I look in the mirror and someone slightly different looks back.
**Under nose:** I did not expect to have these feelings about hair.
**Chin:** Part of me thinks I should not care.
**Collarbone:** But the hair felt like mine. The change feels like a marker I did not ask for.
**Under arm:** I am allowed to have complicated feelings about my own appearance.
**Top of head:** The feelings do not have to make sense to be real.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** My hair is still mine.
**Side of eye:** It is changing because I am living and time is passing.
**Under eye:** The grey arrived because I am still here.
**Under nose:** There are women who did not get to go grey.
**Chin:** My changing hair is evidence of a continuing life.
**Collarbone:** I can decide what to do with it from a place of choice, not fear.
**Under arm:** Grey or not grey is mine to decide.
**Top of head:** My hair is mine. My age is mine. My life is mine.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
